response.justification: The candidate is a versatile polyglot engineer with a genuine developer-tooling streak — he shipped C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI (an actual IDE) and authored test runners and assertion libraries — but the role's core requirements are largely absent. His Java exposure is minimal and dated, there is no Kotlin, no Spring or enterprise JVM framework experience, and no evidence of deep JVM internals knowledge. His recent decade is TypeScript/Node and DLang work, which does not translate into the required expertise for building enterprise Java/Kotlin tooling.
strengths: Hands-on IDE product experience building C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI
Authored developer tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts, MCP code generators), showing real interest in language tooling
Experience with complex, high-throughput systems (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests per second)
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only brief, peripheral Java usage; no demonstrated deep JVM understanding
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or other enterprise JVM framework experience
Recent roles are TypeScript/Node web-platform work, far from the JVM tooling domain

key_factors.factor: Missing required Java/Kotlin/Spring and JVM depth
